message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer B) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-OPS-1:
The lockfile-sync "reproducibility guard" re-resolved against live PyPI (no --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was failing on clean checkouts (main CI red). Fix: a single committed date file .uv-exclude-newer (2026-06-14T00:00:00Z) is read by BOTH ci.yml (3 compile commands) and scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh, passing --exclude-newer so resolution is pinned to a point-in-time index. The cutoff was chosen clear of a boundary release (arize-phoenix 17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic. All three locks regenerated. VERIFY reproducibility: run the regen and a fresh `uv pip compile ... --exclude-newer "$(cat .uv-exclude-newer | tr -d '[:space:]')"` and confirm the non-comment content matches the committed requirements.lock (uv 0.11.21 is available locally). Tests: test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-OPS-1. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

 .github/workflows/ci.yml                           |   25 +-
 .uv-exclude-newer                                  |    1 +
 pyproject.toml                                     |   10 +-
 requirements-build.lock                            |    2 +-
 requirements-core.lock                             |    2 +-

./scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh
---CI---
./.github/workflows/ci.yml

.../agent-aac19b45282b84e77.meta.json              |    1 +
 .../wf_496c86b1-0fc/agent-ab942d28ff63da34f.jsonl  |   22 +

 .../agent-ab9e88ac4245119e1.meta.json              |    1 +

 .../workflows/wf_496c86b1-0fc/journal.jsonl        |   50 +
 .../tool-results/b1lff01wm.txt                     | 4737 ++++++++++++++++++

 .../workflows/wf_496c86b1-0fc.json                 |    1 +
 98 files changed, 17297 insertions(+), 224 deletions(-)

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
3	#
4	# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
5	# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
6	# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
7	# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
8	#
9	#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
10	#
11	# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
12	# The resolution is ALSO pinned to a fixed point-in-time index via --exclude-newer (#K-OPS-1): the
13	# date lives in .uv-exclude-newer (single source of truth, read by CI too) so the regen is
14	# deterministic. Without it, uv resolves the NEWEST in-range version of every (largely
15	# unconstrained, transitive) dep at wall-clock time, so the committed lock would drift the instant
16	# any upstream publishes an in-range release and the "in sync" gate would go red on unrelated PRs.
17	# To intentionally pick up new releases, bump the date in .uv-exclude-newer and re-run this script.
18	#
19	# This regenerates ALL THREE gated lockfiles — requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, AND
20	# requirements-build.lock (#K-OPS-2): the build lock is gated by CI too, so omitting it left a
21	# maintainer following this script still red on a build-backend bump with no scripted fix.
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."
25	
26	UV_VERSION="0.11.21"
27	EXCLUDE_NEWER="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)"
28	
29	if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
30	  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
31	  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
32	fi
33	
34	uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes \
35	  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements.lock
36	uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes \
37	  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-core.lock
38	printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
39	uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes \
40	  --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o requirements-build.lock
41	
42	echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock + requirements-build.lock (exclude-newer=$EXCLUDE_NEWER) — review and commit them."
43

content.text: Now let me look at the CI workflow and the regression test.

content.content:
1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
9	# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
10	# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
11	permissions:
12	  contents: read
13	
14	jobs:
15	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
16	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
17	  core:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    strategy:
20	      matrix:
21	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
22	    steps:
23	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
24	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
25	        with:
26	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
27	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
28	        run: |
29	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
30	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
31	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
32	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
36	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
37	      - name: Lint (ruff)
38	        run: ruff check src tests
39	      - name: Format check (ruff)
40	        run: ruff format --check src tests
41	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
42	        run: mypy
43	      - name: Test (pytest)
44	        run: pytest -q
45	
46	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
47	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
48	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
49	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
50	  # e2e) run for real.
51	  integration:
52	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
53	    services:
54	      postgres:
55	        image: postgres:16
56	        env:
57	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
58	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
59	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
60	        options: >-
61	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
62	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
63	      qdrant:
64	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
65	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
66	    env:
67	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
68	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
69	    steps:
70	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
71	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
72	        with:
73	          python-version: "3.12"
74	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
75	        run: |
76	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
77	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
78	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
79	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
80	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
81	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
82	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
83	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
84	        run: mypy
85	      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
86	        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
87	        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
88	        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
89	        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
90	        run: |
91	          set -euo pipefail
92	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
93	            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
94	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
95	          done
96	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
97	            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
98	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
99	          done
100	          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
101	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
102	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
103	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
104	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
105	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
106	        # --cov-fail-under ENFORCES a floor (#K-OPS-3): without it `pytest --cov` prints coverage
107	        # but exits 0 at any level, so the coverage "gate" was vacuous — a refactor deleting tests
108	        # or adding a large untested module would still pass green. The floor (90) sits below the
109	        # current ~97% with headroom for environment-dependent skips; ratchet it up over time.
110	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90
111	
112	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
113	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
114	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
115	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
116	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
117	  audit:
118	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
119	    steps:
120	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
121	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
122	        with:
123	          python-version: "3.12"
124	      - name: Install pip-audit
125	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
126	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
127	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
128	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
129	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
130	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
131	        run: |
132	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
133	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
134	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
135	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
136	          # Pin the RESOLUTION to a fixed point-in-time index (#K-OPS-1). Without --exclude-newer,
137	          # uv resolves the newest in-range version of every (largely unconstrained, transitive)
138	          # dep at CI wall-clock time, so this "reproducibility" gate goes red the instant ANY
139	          # upstream publishes an in-range release — on PRs that touch no dependency. The date is
140	          # the single source of truth in .uv-exclude-newer (regenerate_lockfiles.sh reads the same
141	          # file), so the regen here matches the committed lock exactly. Bump that file to update.
142	          EXCLUDE_NEWER="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .uv-exclude-newer)"
143	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
144	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
145	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
146	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
147	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
148	          # stay in sync too.
149	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
150	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
151	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
152	          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
153	          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
154	          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
155	          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes --exclude-newer "$EXCLUDE_NEWER" -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
156	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
157	            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh"; exit 1; }
158	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
159	        run: |
160	          set -euo pipefail
161	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
162	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
163	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
164	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
165	          IGNORES=()
166	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
167	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
168	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
169	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
170	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
171	          fi
172	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
173	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
174	        run: |
175	          set -euo pipefail
176	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
177	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
178	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
179	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
180	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
181	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
182	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
183	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
184	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
185	          IGNORES=()
186	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
187	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
188	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
189	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
190	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
191	          fi
192	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
193	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
194	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
195	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
196	        if: always()
197	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
198	        with:
199	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
200	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
201	          if-no-files-found: error
202	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
203	        run: |
204	          set -euo pipefail
205	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
206	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
207	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
208	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
209	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
210	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
211	          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
212	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
213	          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
214	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
215	      - name: Upload license report
216	        if: always()
217	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
218	        with:
219	          name: license-report
220	          path: licenses.json
221	          if-no-files-found: warn
222	
223	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
224	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
225	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
226	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
227	  all-checks:
228	    if: always()
229	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
230	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
231	    steps:
232	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
233	        run: |
234	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
235	          echo "job results: $results"
236	          for r in $results; do
237	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
238	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
239	              exit 1
240	            fi
241	          done
242	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"
243

content.text:
All verification complete. Let me assess one potential gap: the test only checks `--exclude-newer` is present as a substring and the date file exists/starts with a digit — it does NOT verify the locks actually reproduce, nor that CI and the script use the SAME date source. But I verified reproducibility manually. Let me note the residual considerations.

Findings summary:

**Appropriate (root cause / approach / layer):** Yes.
- Root cause correctly identified: `uv pip compile` without `--exclude-newer` resolves newest in-range versions at wall-clock time, so the "in sync" gate goes red whenever any unconstrained transitive publishes — exactly the failure described.
- Approach is the canonical fix: pin resolution to a point-in-time index via `--exclude-newer`.
- Single source of truth (`.uv-exclude-newer`) read by both ci.yml and the regen script via identical `tr -d '[:space:]'` parsing — CI and script cannot drift.
- Right layer: the date file + CI + regen script, not a band-aid.

**Complete (reproducibility verified):** Yes.
- All three locks regenerate byte-identical (non-comment content) at cutoff `2026-06-14T00:00:00Z` with uv 0.11.21: `requirements.lock`, `requirements-core.lock`, `requirements-build.lock` all produced `MATCH`.
- The date file is actually committed (`git ls-files` confirms; `git show d08f031:.uv-exclude-newer` returns the value).

**Discrimination verified:** Yes — simulated revert (stripped `--exclude-newer` from ci.yml + removed date file) makes `test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned` FAIL; restored tree passes. Working tree confirmed clean after.

**Residual gaps (minor, do not rise to CONCERN):**
- The regression test is a static/structural assertion (substring `--exclude-newer` present + date file exists + starts with a digit). It does not itself execute a uv compile to prove the lock reproduces, nor assert CI and the script reference the same file. So a future edit that, say, used a different hardcoded date in one place, or removed the flag from only one of the three CI compile commands, would not be caught by the test (the test only checks the flag appears once in the whole ci.yml text). This is a test-strength limitation, not a defect in the K-OPS-1 fix itself — I confirmed reproducibility by hand, and the fix wires a single shared source.
- The cutoff is static; new in-range advisories published after 2026-06-14 won't be picked up until someone bumps the date (this is the intended, documented tradeoff, and Dependabot covers advisory monitoring).

The fix is appropriate and complete; reproducibility and discrimination both verified empirically.

input.rationale: Root cause is correctly identified and fixed at the right layer. The lockfile sync gate previously ran `uv pip compile` with no `--exclude-newer`, resolving newest in-range transitives at wall-clock time, so the "in sync" gate went red the instant any unconstrained dep published — the exact described clean-checkout failure. The fix pins resolution to a committed point-in-time index (.uv-exclude-newer = 2026-06-14T00:00:00Z) read by BOTH ci.yml (all three compile commands) and scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh via identical `tr -d '[:space:]'` parsing, so CI and the script cannot drift. I empirically verified reproducibility with uv 0.11.21: all three locks (requirements.lock, requirements-core.lock, requirements-build.lock) regenerate byte-identical (non-comment content) at the committed cutoff. The date file is genuinely committed (git ls-files + git show on the fix commit). Discrimination verified: simulating a revert (stripping --exclude-newer from ci.yml + removing the date file) makes test_lockfile_sync_is_resolution_pinned FAIL; with the fix it passes. Working tree restored clean afterward; both regression tests pass.
concerns: Test is structural only: it asserts the substring --exclude-newer appears somewhere in ci.yml (not in each of the three compile commands) and that the date file exists/starts with a digit. It does not execute a uv compile to prove reproducibility, nor assert CI and the regen script reference the SAME date file. A future edit that dropped the flag from only one of the three CI commands, or hardcoded a divergent date in one place, would not be caught by this test. This is a test-strength limitation, not a defect in the K-OPS-1 fix; reproducibility was confirmed manually here.

The cutoff is static, so in-range releases/advisories published after 2026-06-14 are not picked up until the date is bumped — the intended, documented tradeoff (Dependabot handles advisory monitoring).

content.text:
Verification complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED.

K-OPS-1 is both appropriate and complete. I verified reproducibility empirically with uv 0.11.21 — all three lockfiles regenerate byte-identical at the committed cutoff — and proved the regression test discriminates (fails on simulated revert, passes with the fix). Working tree left clean. Two minor non-blocking concerns recorded around test strength and the static cutoff tradeoff.
